meta.name: Kimchi Jjigae (Korean Kimchi Stew)
meta.subtitle: Spicy, savory stew built on well-fermented kimchi, aromatic pork (optional), and tofu.

source.name: ssa.catalogs.cuisines.korean
source.notes: Canonical SSA kimchi jjigae with optional pork, tuna, and vegetarian variations; includes consistency checks and safety notes.

yield.notes: Makes ~6 cups (1.4 L). Serve with steamed rice and banchan.

notes: funky
tangy
deep porky (if used)
rich chili

optional.label: Ttukbaegi (Korean earthenware stew pot)
optional.notes: Classic serving vessel; holds heat well.

notes: Kimchi often contains fish sauce/shrimp paste; choose vegan kimchi if needed.
Use gluten-free gochujang/soy sauce if sensitive to wheat.

ingredients.label: Well-fermented kimchi, chopped
ingredients.notes: About 3 cups. Older/sour kimchi makes better jjigae.

ingredients.label: Kimchi juice
ingredients.notes: Add for extra depth; reduce if kimchi is extremely salty.

ingredients.label: Pork shoulder or pork belly, bite-size
ingredients.notes: Classic. Substitute tuna (see variations) or omit for vegetarian.

ingredients.label: Gochujang (Korean chili paste)
ingredients.notes: Optional but common: adds body and sweetness.

ingredients.label: Toasted sesame oil
ingredients.notes: A few drops at the end is traditional.

ingredients.label: Anchovy-kelp stock (or chicken stock / water)
ingredients.notes: Traditional is anchovy-kelp (dashima + anchovy). Chicken stock works; water is acceptable with seasoning.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Adjust at end; kimchi varies in saltiness.

ingredients.label: Doenjang (Korean soybean paste)
ingredients.notes: Optional: small amount rounds out funk and depth.

notes: If using pork, cook until fully done (no pink).
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat to a steady simmer before serving.

fridge.notes: Flavor improves on day 2. Store in sealed container.

freezer.notes: Not recommended; tofu texture changes after freezing.

notes: Reheat on stove to simmer 5–8 minutes, adding a splash of water/stock if thickened.
Replace pork with 1–2 cans tuna (drained). Add tuna in the last 5–7 minutes of simmering to avoid drying it out.
Use vegan kimchi and water/vegetable stock.
Add mushrooms (shiitake/king oyster) for body, and consider a bit more doenjang for umami (if diet allows).

swap.notes: Add 250–300 g mushrooms; sauté until browned before adding stock.

notes: Omit gochugaru and reduce gochujang to 1 tsp (or omit). Let the kimchi provide the flavor.
Brown pork well and sauté kimchi longer (5–7 minutes) before adding stock.
Use anchovy-kelp stock for classic depth.

variations.label: Add tteok (rice cakes)
notes: Add sliced rice cakes during the last 8–10 minutes of simmering until tender.

variations.label: Add enoki mushrooms
notes: Add enoki in the last 3 minutes to keep them delicate and springy.

variations.label: Add cheese (modern comfort style)
notes: Top with a slice of mozzarella/cheddar in the last minute to melt. Not traditional, but popular.
The key is sour, well-fermented kimchi—fresh kimchi makes a flatter stew.
Kimchi saltiness varies widely; season at the end after simmering.
If you want a deeper broth without anchovy stock, a small spoon of doenjang helps.

steps.title: Prep ingredients
instructions: Chop kimchi into bite-size pieces and reserve kimchi juice if using.
Slice onion and chop scallions (separate whites and greens if you want a cleaner finish).
Mince garlic.
Slice tofu into 1–2 cm slabs.
checks: All ingredients are ready before cooking starts.

steps.title: Brown pork (optional, recommended)
instructions: Heat neutral oil in a pot over medium-high.
If using pork, add it in a single layer and cook until lightly browned on most sides (3–5 minutes).
Add onion and cook 1–2 minutes until slightly softened.
checks: Pork has browned edges (foundation for deeper broth).

steps.title: Sauté kimchi and aromatics
instructions: Add chopped kimchi to the pot (with onion/pork if used).
Cook 3–5 minutes, stirring, until kimchi looks slightly softened and fragrant.
Add garlic and gochugaru (if using) and cook 30 seconds.
Stir in gochujang and/or doenjang (if using) until dissolved into the pot juices.
checks: Kimchi is fragrant; chili paste is evenly mixed (no clumps).

steps.title: Add stock and simmer
instructions: Add stock (or water) and kimchi juice (if using).
Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Simmer 15–20 minutes to develop flavor.
checks: Broth tastes developed: tangy, savory, and balanced (before final seasoning).

steps.title: Add tofu and finish
instructions: Gently add sliced tofu and simmer 5 minutes.
Add scallions and simmer 1–2 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning: a splash of soy sauce if needed; salt only if necessary.
Finish with a few drops of sesame oil (optional) and black pepper (optional).
checks: Tofu is heated through but not broken apart.
Seasoning is adjusted after simmering (not before).

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with steamed rice.
Offer extra kimchi, gim (roasted seaweed), or banchan on the side.
checks: Stew is served piping hot; rice is ready alongside.
